Catha edulis, commonly known as the Catevala or Khat plant, is a flowering plant native to the Horn of Africa and the Arabian Peninsula. The leaves of the Catevala plant contain bioactive constituents such as cathinone, cathine, and catharanthine, which are known for their potential health benefits, including reducing anxiety and improving cognitive function. The health benefits of Catevala also include reducing fatigue, improving mood, and aiding in weight loss. The most common herbal preparations made with Catevala include tea, powder, and chewing sticks, which are traditionally used in traditional medicine practices.
